name: Systematic review found urolithin A human muscle and biomarker signals but no physical-function benefit
summary: The urolithin A review helps bound the track's human-functional stage assignment.
statement: A 2024 systematic review of five human urolithin A studies with 250 healthy participants found dose-dependent anti-inflammatory effects, mitochondrial/autophagy/fatty-acid-oxidation biomarker movement, and muscle strength or endurance signals, but no effect on anthropometrics, cardiovascular outcomes, or physical function.
population_or_model: Healthy human participants across five urolithin A studies
quantitative_note: The review summarizes 10 to 1000 mg/day urolithin A across five studies and 250 healthy individuals.
caveats: This is a secondary synthesis, not an additional primary trial.
The review reinforces mixed functional interpretation: muscle-test signals are not the same as broader physical function.
Longer and more physiologically diverse trials were identified as needed.
